items.title: Open-source Vulkan driver NVK gains experimental DLSS support — bringing Nvidia’s upscaling tech to Linux via imported CUDA binaries
items.description: NVK, the community-built open-source Vulkan driver for Nvidia GPUs in Mesa, has gained experimental DLSS support.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter NVK, the community-built open-source Vulkan driver for Nvidia GPUs in Mesa, has gained experimental DLSS support, with the code landing in Mesa 26.2-devel, as reported by Phoronix. The driver doesn’t reimplement the upscaler but instead loads Nvidia's own pre-compiled CUDA binaries and runs them, a workaround that keeps the feature behind an experimental flag and ties it to whether compatible bytecode exists for a given card. Nvidia's proprietary Linux driver has of course handled DLSS for years, so the change closes one of the bigger gaps between the closed driver and its open-source counterpart, rather than bringing the technology to Linux for the first time.DLSS runs on NVK through VK_NVX_binary_import, a Vulkan extension that lets an application load Nvidia CuBIN files, the pre-baked CUDA binaries Nvidia, and loads them on the GPU. Autumn Ashton opened the original pull request for the extension last year, and Thomas Andersen revived it roughly two months ago to clear merge conflicts and finish the work, with the path sitting behind the NVK_EXPERIMENTAL=dlss environment variable because known bugs remain.The catch is the reliance on pre-compiled binaries; NVK can only run DLSS where compatible bytecode already exists for the GPU in use. The proprietary Nvidia driver avoids that limit with a route that compiles PTX, Nvidia's intermediate assembly, down to GPU bytecode at runtime. NVK has no equivalent, because it can’t translate Nvidia PTX into NIR, which is the intermediate representation Mesa drivers compile from.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Support for DLSS across the broader Linux graphics stack has been uneven, to say the least. As of late last year, Nvidia's DLSS 4 was still unsupported in Valve's VKD3D-Proton translation layer, which converts DirectX 12 calls to Vulkan for games running through Proton.NVK began in 2022 as a from-scratch Vulkan driver led by Collabora's Faith Ekstrand alongside Karol Herbst and Dave Airlie at Red Hat, and it supports Turing (RTX 20-series and GTX 16-series) and newer architectures. In late 2024, it became the first open-source Vulkan driver for Nvidia hardware to pass Khronos conformance, reaching Vulkan 1.4 provisional spec. It runs on the Nouveau kernel driver and is separate from Nvidia's own open-source kernel modules, which the company ships with its proprietary user-space software stack. You may like Nvidia DLSS 4.5 Dynamic MFG plus 5X and 6X framegen modes enter beta for RTX 50-series users GitHub user creates open-source Nvidia GeForce Now client alternative DLSS 4.5 Ray Reconstruction update arrives in August for better ray tracing visuals At the XDC2025 conference in November, Ekstrand said NVK runs at around 50% of the official Nvidia driver's speed in many titles, that ray tracing is still in progress, and that the team is "barely keeping the lights on" with current developer resources, according to Phoronix. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 开源Vulkan驱动程序NVK新增实验性DLSS支持——借助导入的CUDA二进制文件将NVIDIA的升频技术引入Linux
items.descriptionZh:
由社区为NVIDIA GPU打造的开源Vulkan驱动NVK（集成于Mesa图形栈）现已获得对DLSS（深度学习超级采样）的实验性支持。该代码已合并至Mesa 26.2-devel分支。NVK并未重新实现DLSS算法，而是另辟蹊径：它直接加载NVIDIA官方预编译的CUDA二进制文件（CuBIN）并驱动其运行。这种变通方案使得该功能被置于实验标志之后，且仅当所用显卡存在兼容的预编译代码时才能启用。NVIDIA官方的Linux闭源驱动早已支持DLSS多年，因此这一更新填补了闭源驱动与开源驱动之间的一个较大差距，但并非首次将DLSS带到Linux平台。

DLSS在NVK上的运行依赖VK_NVX_binary_import扩展——这是Vulkan的一个扩展接口，允许应用程序将NVIDIA CuBIN文件（即预制的CUDA二进制）加载到GPU上执行。这一扩展的原始合并请求由Autumn Ashton在去年提出，随后Thomas Andersen在约两个月前接手，解决了合并冲突并完成收尾工作。目前该功能默认关闭，需要通过设置环境变量`NVK_EXPERIMENTAL=dlss`来启用，原因是仍存在已知的bug。

关键在于对预编译二进制的依赖：NVK只能运行那些已经为当前GPU预先生成好兼容字节码的DLSS版本。相比之下，NVIDIA官方闭源驱动通过运行时编译绕过这一限制：它将PTX（NVIDIA的中间汇编语言）即时编译为GPU原生字节码。NVK目前没有等效机制，因为它无法将NVIDIA的PTX翻译成Mesa驱动所编译的中间表示（NIR）。

从更广泛的Linux图形栈来看，对DLSS的支持一直参差不齐。截至去年底，NVIDIA的DLSS 4在Valve的VKD3D-Proton转换层中依然不支持——该层负责将DirectX 12调用转换为Vulkan，以便在Proton上运行Windows游戏。

NVK项目始于2022年，由Collabora的Faith Ekstrand联合Red Hat的Karol Herbst和Dave Airlie从零开始构建，支持Turing架构（RTX 20系列和GTX 16系列）及更新型号。2024年底，它成为首个通过Khronos一致性测试的NVIDIA硬件开源Vulkan驱动，达到了Vulkan 1.4临时规范。NVK运行于Nouveau内核驱动之上，与NVIDIA公司随其闭源用户态软件栈提供的开源内核模块是各自独立的。

在2025年11月的XDC2025会议上，据Phoronix报道，Ekstrand表示NVK在众多游戏中的性能大约只有官方NVIDIA驱动速度的50%，光线追踪支持仍在开发中，开发团队目前“仅勉强维持运转”，资源十分紧张。

总体而言，NVK的DLSS实验性支持标志着开源NVIDIA驱动生态的重要一步，让Linux用户在无需闭源驱动的情况下也能体验到NVIDIA的AI超分辨率技术。不过，由于依赖预编译二进制文件、性能差距较大以及开发资源有限，该功能目前尚不具备日常使用的成熟度，但它为社区驱动的图形栈探索了一条可行的技术路径。

items.title: Dramatically redesigned GMKtec EVO-X3 shown bearing Lisa Su’s signature of approval — flagship AI mini PC workstation is built around AMD’s Ryzen AI Max+ 395 'Strix Halo' processor, again
items.description: GMKtec's dramatically redesigned EVO-X3 'Strix Halo' Mini PC gets Lisa Su’s signature of approval.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Mini PC specialist GMKtec recently launched the EVO-X3, heralding it as a “next-gen AI workstation,” with early access registration beginning tomorrow (Monday, June 22). The firm also took to social media this weekend to show that the successor to the GMKtec EVO-X2, which we reviewed in February, had earned AMD CEO Lisa Su’s signature of approval. This is not entirely surprising, as so did the X2. So, let’s take a closer look at the new GMKtec EVO-X3.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: GMKtec)(Image credit: GMKtec)(Image credit: GMKtec)Actually, the tech specs of the EVO-X3 aren’t very different from its predecessor. We can say with certainty that its design has been thoroughly revamped, though. Look at the difference between the generations. This is a good thing, as most of our reviewers ‘Cons’ regarding the EVO-X2 were drawn from its design and build. Specifically we grumbled about the cheap-feeling case, tricky internal access, and fan noise. A smart redesign would have addressed all these points, but we’d have to get some hands-on time with the new revision to be sure it delivers more than a cosmetic change.The new GMKtec EVO-X3 is again built around the AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 'Strix Halo' processor. Interestingly, a switch to the Ryzen AI Max+ 495 'Gorgon Halo' must have been deemed counterproductive. Many tech enthusiasts were underwhelmed by the AMD Ryzen AI 400 series refresh, too. We also know there are two launch configurations, both with 128GB of LPDDR5X-8000 RAM, but one having a 2TB SSD, the other 4TB. The machine is advertised as featuring two M.2 2280 PCIe Gen4x4 slots for up to 8TB of SSD storage.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: As hinted above, the big change between generations appears to be in the build. GMKtec now boasts of the “silent triple fan thermal system” which makes the whole unit somewhat like a triple fan graphics card in a steel wrapper. GMKtec says that “The system is engineered to balance performance, efficiency, and thermal stability, making it suitable for continuous professional workloads across AI and creative scenarios.”For ports, we note the EVO-X3 packs in an OCuLink connector, USB4 for data, power delivery and video out, and HDMI 2.1 video out. Wi-Fi 7 and Bluetooth 5.4 are also on board. There are also a few USB-A ports, an Ethernet jack, and a headset jack. You may like AMD challenges Nvidia's DGX Spark with $3,999 Ryzen AI Halo with Windows 11 support Corsair's Strix Halo AI Workstation 300 gets even more expensive amid the RAMpocalypse AMD Ryzen AI Max 400 ‘Gorgon Halo’ packs up to 192GB of unified memory Image 1 of 2(Image credit: GMKtec)(Image credit: GMKtec)Prices up, a lotThe GMKtec EVO-X2 launched at $1,499 as tested with 64GB RAM and 1TB SSD. Today, EVO-X2 machines with this configuration are currently being sold for $1,999 on Amazon.com. However, the GMKtec EVO-X3 entry price is far steeper, admittedly with more RAM and storage. It is advertising the 128GB RAM and 2TB SSD version at $3,600, and the 128GB RAM plus 4TB storage version at $3,849. Both of those are said to be pre-launch discounted prices. Signing up for the early access registration can get you an additional $20 off those marked-down prices.GMKtec told us that the pre-launch is live tomorrow, with purchased units being sent out from July 6.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Mark Tyson
items.titleZh: 大幅重新设计的GMKtec EVO-X3亮相，带有苏姿丰的签名背书——旗舰级AI迷你PC工作站再次搭载AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 'Strix Halo'处理器
items.descriptionZh:
迷你PC专业厂商GMKtec近日发布了新一代“下一代AI工作站”EVO-X3，并宣布早期访问注册将于次日（6月22日周一）启动。该机作为此前评测过的EVO-X2的继任者，已获得AMD CEO Lisa Su的签名认可——这一荣誉在X2上也曾出现。我们来看看这款新品的具体信息。

**设计与核心架构的革新**

EVO-X3最显著的变化在于其工业设计。与X2相比，整机造型经过彻底重造。此前的X2评测中，我们曾抱怨其廉价感的外壳、复杂的内部拆装以及风扇噪音问题。新款通过“静音三风扇散热系统”解决了这些痛点——这相当于为整机加装了类似高端显卡的三风扇主动散热模组，并用钢质外壳包裹。GMKtec宣称该系统“旨在平衡性能、能效与热稳定性”，使之能胜任AI运算和创意场景下的持续专业负载。

硬件方面，EVO-X3依旧搭载AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 “Strix Halo”处理器，而非升级至传闻中的“Gorgon Halo”系列。这可能是考虑到Ryzen AI 400系列（Gorgon Halo）在技术圈反响平平。内存配置为128GB LPDDR5X-8000，提供2TB SSD和4TB SSD两种首发配置，且均支持双M.2 2280 PCIe Gen4x4插槽，最高可扩展至8TB。

**性能参数与连接性**

接口方面，EVO-X3配备了OCuLink连接器（用于外接显卡扩展）、支持数据/供电/视频传输的USB4、HDMI 2.1视频输出、Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙5.4，以及若干USB-A接口、一个以太网口和一个耳机插孔。这些配置使其能轻松融入AI工作站、高性能计算或创意工作流。

**价格与市场定位**

价格方面，EVO-X3的起售价大幅提升。作为对比，此前EVO-X2（64GB RAM+1TB SSD）上市价为1499美元，如今同配置在亚马逊上已涨至1999美元。而EVO-X3的128GB+2TB版本预售价为3600美元，128GB+4TB版本为3849美元——这已是折扣价，早期注册用户还可再减20美元。预购将于明日启动，7月6日起发货。

**技术影响与应用场景**

EVO-X3的定位明确指向“AI工作站”。其核心卖点在于：1）统一内存架构（128GB LPDDR5X）允许AI模型直接在内存中运行，避免显存瓶颈；2）Strix Halo处理器的集成RDNA 3+ GPU可加速大规模并行计算和推理任务；3）三风扇散热系统保障长时间稳定运行。适合用于本地大语言模型部署、3D渲染、视频编辑、科学计算等场景。

值得注意的是，GMKtec官方宣称该机型可“支持Windows 11的AMD AI Halo平台”，暗示其与微软生态的深度绑定。此外，由于定价达到3000-4000美元区间，它直接对标苹果Mac Studio（顶配）和NVIDIA DGX Spark等竞品，但凭借AMD的x86兼容性和可扩展存储，在特定专业用户群体中仍有竞争力。

总体来看，EVO-X3是GMKtec在高端迷你工作站领域的又一次尝试，设计改进值得肯定，但高昂的定价能否获得市场认可，仍需等待后续评测和用户反馈。

history.title: Open-source Vulkan driver NVK gains experimental DLSS support — bringing Nvidia’s upscaling tech to Linux via imported CUDA binaries
history.description: NVK, the community-built open-source Vulkan driver for Nvidia GPUs in Mesa, has gained experimental DLSS support.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter NVK, the community-built open-source Vulkan driver for Nvidia GPUs in Mesa, has gained experimental DLSS support, with the code landing in Mesa 26.2-devel, as reported by Phoronix. Nvidia's proprietary Linux driver has of course handled DLSS for years, so the change closes one of the bigger gaps between the closed driver and its open-source counterpart, rather than bringing the technology to Linux for the first time.DLSS runs on NVK through VK_NVX_binary_import, a Vulkan extension that lets an application load Nvidia CuBIN files, the pre-baked CUDA binaries Nvidia, and loads them on the GPU. Autumn Ashton opened the original pull request for the extension last year, and Thomas Andersen revived it roughly two months ago to clear merge conflicts and finish the work, with the path sitting behind the NVK_EXPERIMENTAL=dlss environment variable because known bugs remain.The catch is the reliance on pre-compiled binaries; NVK can only run DLSS where compatible bytecode already exists for the GPU in use. The proprietary Nvidia driver avoids that limit with a route that compiles PTX, Nvidia's intermediate assembly, down to GPU bytecode at runtime. NVK has no equivalent, because it can’t translate Nvidia PTX into NIR, which is the intermediate representation Mesa drivers compile from.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Support for DLSS across the broader Linux graphics stack has been uneven, to say the least. As of late last year, Nvidia's DLSS 4 was still unsupported in Valve's VKD3D-Proton translation layer, which converts DirectX 12 calls to Vulkan for games running through Proton.NVK began in 2022 as a from-scratch Vulkan driver led by Collabora's Faith Ekstrand alongside Karol Herbst and Dave Airlie at Red Hat, and it supports Turing (RTX 20-series and GTX 16-series) and newer architectures. history.title: Dramatically redesigned GMKtec EVO-X3 shown bearing Lisa Su’s signature of approval — flagship AI mini PC workstation is built around AMD’s Ryzen AI Max+ 395 'Strix Halo' processor, again

history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Mini PC specialist GMKtec recently launched the EVO-X3, heralding it as a “next-gen AI workstation,” with early access registration beginning tomorrow (Monday, June 22). Today, EVO-X2 machines with this configuration are currently being sold for $1,999 on Amazon.com. However, the GMKtec EVO-X3 entry price is far steeper, admittedly with more RAM and storage. It is advertising the 128GB RAM and 2TB SSD version at $3,600, and the 128GB RAM plus 4TB storage version at $3,849. Both of those are said to be pre-launch discounted prices. Signing up for the early access registration can get you an additional $20 off those marked-down prices.GMKtec told us that the pre-launch is live tomorrow, with purchased units being sent out from July 6.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Mark Tyson
history.titleZh: 大幅重新设计的GMKtec EVO-X3亮相，带有苏姿丰的签名背书——旗舰级AI迷你PC工作站再次搭载AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 'Strix Halo'处理器
history.descriptionZh:
迷你PC专业厂商GMKtec近日发布了新一代“下一代AI工作站”EVO-X3，并宣布早期访问注册将于次日（6月22日周一）启动。该机作为此前评测过的EVO-X2的继任者，已获得AMD CEO Lisa Su的签名认可——这一荣誉在X2上也曾出现。我们来看看这款新品的具体信息。

**设计与核心架构的革新**

EVO-X3最显著的变化在于其工业设计。与X2相比，整机造型经过彻底重造。此前的X2评测中，我们曾抱怨其廉价感的外壳、复杂的内部拆装以及风扇噪音问题。新款通过“静音三风扇散热系统”解决了这些痛点——这相当于为整机加装了类似高端显卡的三风扇主动散热模组，并用钢质外壳包裹。GMKtec宣称该系统“旨在平衡性能、能效与热稳定性”，使之能胜任AI运算和创意场景下的持续专业负载。

硬件方面，EVO-X3依旧搭载AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 “Strix Halo”处理器，而非升级至传闻中的“Gorgon Halo”系列。这可能是考虑到Ryzen AI 400系列（Gorgon Halo）在技术圈反响平平。内存配置为128GB LPDDR5X-8000，提供2TB SSD和4TB SSD两种首发配置，且均支持双M.2 2280 PCIe Gen4x4插槽，最高可扩展至8TB。

**性能参数与连接性**

接口方面，EVO-X3配备了OCuLink连接器（用于外接显卡扩展）、支持数据/供电/视频传输的USB4、HDMI 2.1视频输出、Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙5.4，以及若干USB-A接口、一个以太网口和一个耳机插孔。这些配置使其能轻松融入AI工作站、高性能计算或创意工作流。

**价格与市场定位**

价格方面，EVO-X3的起售价大幅提升。作为对比，此前EVO-X2（64GB RAM+1TB SSD）上市价为1499美元，如今同配置在亚马逊上已涨至1999美元。而EVO-X3的128GB+2TB版本预售价为3600美元，128GB+4TB版本为3849美元——这已是折扣价，早期注册用户还可再减20美元。预购将于明日启动，7月6日起发货。

**技术影响与应用场景**

EVO-X3的定位明确指向“AI工作站”。其核心卖点在于：1）统一内存架构（128GB LPDDR5X）允许AI模型直接在内存中运行，避免显存瓶颈；2）Strix Halo处理器的集成RDNA 3+ GPU可加速大规模并行计算和推理任务；3）三风扇散热系统保障长时间稳定运行。适合用于本地大语言模型部署、3D渲染、视频编辑、科学计算等场景。

值得注意的是，GMKtec官方宣称该机型可“支持Windows 11的AMD AI Halo平台”，暗示其与微软生态的深度绑定。此外，由于定价达到3000-4000美元区间，它直接对标苹果Mac Studio（顶配）和NVIDIA DGX Spark等竞品，但凭借AMD的x86兼容性和可扩展存储，在特定专业用户群体中仍有竞争力。

总体来看，EVO-X3是GMKtec在高端迷你工作站领域的又一次尝试，设计改进值得肯定，但高昂的定价能否获得市场认可，仍需等待后续评测和用户反馈。
